headlines.title: India Resumes Iraqi Oil Imports Despite Hormuz Shipping Risks
headlines.summary: Indian state refiners are back to buying Iraqi crude that would have to transit the Strait of Hormuz, in a sign that the world’s third-largest crude oil importer seeks to bring supply from the Middle East after the tentative reopening of the chokepoint.
